from pyspark.sql import functions as F

SOURCE_FILE = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-SPREADSHEETS/MPESC-SPREADSHEETS-RAW/Address.csv"

load_file = (
    spark.read
        .format("csv")
        .option("header", True)
        .option("sep", ",")
        .option("multiLine", True)          
        .option("quote", '"')               # handle quoted fields
        .option("escape", '"')              # handle escaped quotes
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")       # don’t fail on bad rows
        .option("inferSchema", False)       # safer for messy exports
        .load(SOURCE_FILE)
)

display(load_file.limit(10))
print("load_file rows:", load_file.count())

from pyspark.sql.functions import regexp_extract

df = load_file.select(
    regexp_extract("id", r"/(\d+)-", 1).alias("Case_ID"),
    "*"
    
)

display(df.limit(10))

# pull in cases to get GUID
CASES_FILE = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-SPREADSHEETS/MPESC-SPREADSHEETS-TRANSFORM/Cases-Transform"

LOAD_CASES = (
    spark.read
        .format("csv")
        .option("header", True)
        .option("sep", ",")
        .option("multiLine", True)          
        .option("quote", '"')               # handle quoted fields
        .option("escape", '"')              # handle escaped quotes
        .option("mode", "PERMISSIVE")       # don’t fail on bad rows
        .option("inferSchema", False)       # safer for messy exports
        .load(CASES_FILE)
)

display(LOAD_CASES.limit(10))
print("load_cases rows:", LOAD_CASES.count())

load_cases_trimmed = LOAD_CASES.select("Case_ID", "GUID")

df_joined = (
    df.alias("d")
      .join(load_cases_trimmed.alias("lc"), on="Case_ID", how="left")
      .select("lc.GUID","d.*")
)

display(df_joined.limit(10))
